name: 桂林
description: Guilin, 'Forest of Osmanthus', is famous for its stunning karst landscape of limestone peaks rising from the Li River. The city has been inspiring Chinese painters and poets for centuries with its ethereal scenery. Besides natural beauty, it offers cave explorations, cycling along the river, bamboo raft trips, and the vibrant Sun and Moon Pagodas.
highlights: Li River Cruise - Karst landscape from water
Reed Flute Cave - Rainbow-lit underground wonder
Longji Rice Terraces - Dragons backbone terraces

climate.tips: Best visited in spring and autumn. Summer is hot and humid with occasional flooding. Winter mild but can be foggy. 'Guilin rain' - frequent afternoon showers create misty scenery. Pack rain gear.

attractions.name: 七星公园
attractions.description: Guilin's largest park featuring seven peaks arranged like the Big Dipper constellation, with caves, ancient trees, and a small zoo.
attractions.tips: Best combined with a visit to the zoo if traveling with children.

attractions.name: 靖江王府
attractions.description: A 600-year-old Ming dynasty princely mansion and UNESCO World Heritage site, featuring ancient architecture and the famous GUILIN DOCUMENT.
attractions.tips: Morning visit recommended to avoid crowds. Audio guide included in ticket.

attractions.name: 东西巷
attractions.description: A renovated historical district near the city center combining traditional architecture with modern amenities, featuring local snacks and crafts.
attractions.tips: Best visited in evening when illuminated. Great for souvenirs and local snacks.

attractions.name: 十里画廊
attractions.description: A scenic cycling route in Yangshuo passing through rolling hills, rice paddies, and traditional villages with stunning karst views.
attractions.tips: Rent e-bikes in Yangshuo. Start early morning for best light and fewer tourists. Stop at cafes along the route.

attractions.name: 遇龙河
attractions.description: A tributary of the Li River, famous for its peaceful bamboo raft rides through dramatic karst scenery away from the crowds.
attractions.tips: Book through your hotel. Less crowded than Li River. Morning mists most beautiful. Bring sunscreen.

attractions.name: 叠彩山
attractions.description: A hill in northern Guilin with excellent views over the city, named for its layers of colored rock resembling folded silk.
attractions.tips: Combine with Fubo Hill for a morning tour. Wear comfortable shoes. Best visited at sunrise for city views.

attractions.name: 拱极山
attractions.description: A small hill with a natural arch offering panoramic views of Guilin and the surrounding karst landscape.
attractions.tips: Less touristy spot favored by locals. Good for sunrise photography.

attractions.name: 尧山
attractions.description: The highest mountain near Guilin at 909m, offering cable car rides and stunning views of the karst landscape from above.
attractions.tips: Best visited on clear days. Summer best for flower sea. Winter for frost views.

attractions.name: 象山传奇
attractions.description: Evening light show and boat tour around Elephant Trunk Hill featuring 3D projection mapping and cultural performances.
attractions.tips: Book through hotel or online. Rain ponchos provided. Good weather essential for best experience.

attractions.name: 海洋银杏林
attractions.description: A vast ginkgo tree forest that turns golden yellow in autumn, creating stunning scenery contrasting with karst peaks.
attractions.tips: Best visited mid-November. Weekends crowded. Combine with nearby villages.

attractions.name: 高田石窟
attractions.description: Ancient Buddhist cave temples dating back to Tang Dynasty, featuring colorful murals and sculptures carved into karst cliffs.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than other caves. Combine with cycling route to Ten Mile Gallery.

attractions.name: 刘三姐歌圩
attractions.description: An open-air venue on the Li River where visitors can experience traditional Guangxi folk songs and culture.
attractions.tips: Evening shows more lively. Interactive experience with performers.

attractions.name: 伏波山
attractions.description: A hill with a distinctive peak that juts out over the river, featuring ancient caves and excellent views of the city.
attractions.tips: Combine with Diecai Mountain for morning tour. Wear comfortable shoes for climbing.

attractions.name: 漓江渔火
attractions.description: Traditional cormorant fishing demonstration on the Li River with lanterns and folk music.
attractions.tips: Book evening tour including dinner. Best in summer. Bring warm jacket for evening.

attractions.name: 银子岩
attractions.description: A vast karst cave system known for its sparkling stalactites that resemble silver formations.
attractions.address: Maling Village, Yangshuo County, 80km from Guilin
attractions.tips: Combine with Yangshuo cycling. Follow guide for best photo spots. The cave is 2km long.

attractions.name: 龙脊平安寨
attractions.description: The most famous Zhuang village in the Longji Rice Terraces area with stunning views and authentic culture.
attractions.address: Ping'an Village, Longsheng County, 100km from Guilin
attractions.tips: Stay overnight for sunrise. Wear hiking shoes. Hire local guide. Try bamboo rice at village restaurants.

attractions.name: 龙脊古壮寨
attractions.description: Ancient Yao village with 400-year-old wooden architecture and authentic minority customs.
attractions.address: Dazhai Village, Longsheng County, 110km from Guilin
attractions.tips: Less touristy than Ping'an. Try Yao specialty dishes. Experience homemade rice wine.

attractions.name: 大圩古镇
attractions.description: A 1000-year-old Ming and Qing dynasty town on the漓江, once a major trading hub with preserved architecture.
attractions.tips: Best in early morning before tour buses. Try local specialties. Watch traditional crafts.

attractions.name: 阳朔西街
attractions.description: China's first 'foreigner street' with 1400 years of history, now a bustling pedestrian zone with shops and restaurants.
attractions.tips: Avoid Golden Week. Explore side alleys for authentic shops. Try street food at night.

attractions.name: 象山公园
attractions.description: The iconic landmark of Guilin where an elephant drinks from the river with its trunk forming a natural arch.
attractions.tips: Best at sunset for reflection shots. The water view from Shanxian Rd is free. Buy ticket for inside access.

restaurants.name: 老东江米粉
restaurants.description: Legendary rice noodle shop (since 1977) - locals line up daily for breakfast.

restaurants.name: 椿记烧鹅
restaurants.description: Popular local restaurant famous for roast goose and excellent Guangxi cuisine.

restaurants.name: 阿甘酒家
restaurants.description: Popular restaurant with good views and authentic local dishes.

restaurants.name: 芒果帮
restaurants.description: Popular mango juice and dessert shop using Guilin's famous mangoes.

restaurants.name: 小南国家宴
restaurants.description: Popular restaurant serving authentic Guangxi cuisine with beautiful riverside setting.

restaurants.name: 米粉自助
restaurants.description: Budget self-service rice noodle restaurant popular with locals. Unlimited toppings.

restaurants.name: 阳朔西街餐厅
restaurants.description: Yangshuo's famous pedestrian street with hundreds of restaurants serving both local and international cuisine.

restaurants.name: 荔浦芋
restaurants.description: Specialty restaurants featuring Lipu taro dishes - a famous Guangxi ingredient.

restaurants.name: 桂花糕
restaurants.description: Traditional osmanthus cakes and pastries - Guilin's signature sweet, made with osmanthus flowers.

restaurants.name: 阳朔啤酒鱼总店
restaurants.description: The original and most famous beer fish restaurant in Yangshuo, located on West Street.

restaurants.name: 桂林米粉大王
restaurants.description: Popular rice noodle shop known for its authentic taste and generous portions.

restaurants.name: 那山米粉
restaurants.description: Local favorite hidden in a small alley, known for authentic flavors and traditional recipes.

restaurants.name: 漓江鱼馆
restaurants.description: Specializing in fresh fish dishes caught from the Li River, with stunning river views.

restaurants.name: 山芋香农家乐
restaurants.description: Rustic restaurant in Longji area serving local Zhuang and Yao cuisine with terrace views.

restaurants.name: 瑶寨农家菜
restaurants.description: Traditional Yao cuisine in a village setting, known for spicy dishes and fermented flavors.

restaurants.name: 月亮咖啡
restaurants.description: Popular cafe with rooftop seating offering good coffee and Western breakfast options.

restaurants.name: 丝路米粉
restaurants.description: Local breakfast spot famous for its light broth and handmade rice noodles.

restaurants.name: 桂花汤圆店
restaurants.description: Traditional dessert shop specializing in osmanthus-flavored dumplings and sweet soups.

restaurants.name: 漓江人家
restaurants.description: Cozy family restaurant with riverside terrace serving homestyle Guangxi dishes.

restaurants.name: 椿记烧鹅形象店
restaurants.nameEn: Chun Ji Roast Goose Image Store
restaurants.description: Famous for roast goose and excellent service. Popular with locals for celebrations.

restaurants.name: 阿元鱼丸
restaurants.description: Specializing in fish ball dishes made from fresh river fish.

restaurants.name: 桂林肥叔
restaurants.description: Local favorite breakfast spot known for big portions and rich broth.

restaurants.name: 阳朔大师傅啤酒鱼
restaurants.description: Renowned beer fish restaurant with authentic Yangshuo style.

restaurants.name: 漓江又一轩
restaurants.description: Riverside restaurant with elegant environment and quality local dishes.

restaurants.name: 象鼻山下农家乐
restaurants.description: Rustic farm-style restaurant with garden seating and fresh local ingredients.

restaurants.name: 龙脊壮家风味
restaurants.description: Authentic Zhuang cuisine in the rice terraces area with terrace views.

restaurants.name: 阳朔谢大姐啤酒鱼
restaurants.description: Popular female-owned restaurant famous for beer fish, featured on food shows.

restaurants.name: 桂林仔啤酒鱼
restaurants.description: Popular local chain with consistent quality and reasonable prices.

restaurants.name: 渔翁渔火
restaurants.description: Riverside restaurant offering dinner with cormorant fishing show.

restaurants.name: 马家米粉
restaurants.description: Popular breakfast spot with authentic Guilin rice noodles and local crowd.

arrival.from: Major domestic airports, growing international routes
arrival.tips: Airport 28km from city center. Airport bus (hourly, 20¥) to downtown. Taxis available.

arrival.to: Guilin Railway Station / Guilin North Station
arrival.tips: Guilin North for high-speed trains to/from Guangzhou, Shenzhen. Old station for trains to/from Beijing.

bus: Good bus connections between scenic areas
Tourist bus to Yangshuo and Longji
taxi: Didi works but less common than cities
bike: Best way to explore karst scenery
Helmet recommended but not always provided

hotels.name: 桂林香格里拉大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Request river-view rooms for best scenery.

hotels.name: 桂林Club Med地中海俱乐部
hotels.bookingTips: Good for families. Book directly for best rates.

hotels.name: 阳朔悦榕庄
hotels.bookingTips: Best luxury stay in the area. Book spa in advance.

hotels.name: 阳朔花梦居客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Book ahead during peak season. Great for budget travelers.

hotels.name: 桂林漓江大瀑布饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Waterfall show at 20:00 daily. Book river-view room.

hotels.name: 桂林榕湖饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Best location near city center attractions.

hotels.name: 阳朔碧莲江景大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best value in Yangshuo with river views.

hotels.name: 龙脊梯田民宿
hotels.bookingTips: Book early for sunrise views. Shared bathroom common.

payment.description: Standard payment, accepted at most modern venues
tips: Guilin less developed - some places only accept cash
Download both Alipay and WeChat as backup

payment.description: Essential in Guilin - many local restaurants and vendors only accept cash
howToUse: ATMs available but rural area access limited
Bamboo raft operators usually cash only
tips: Longji Rice Terraces area mostly cash
Exchange before heading to rural areas

culturalTips.title: Minority Cultures
culturalTips.content: Guilin area home to Zhuang, Yao, and other minority groups. Longji Rice Terraces are farmed by Zhuang and Yao people. Respect local customs when visiting villages.

culturalTips.title: Weather and Photography
culturalTips.content: Misty weather creates the classic Guilin scenery - don't be disappointed by 'bad' weather. Early morning and evening light most magical. Rain creates mist and reflections.

culturalTips.title: Yangshuo Cycling
culturalTips.content: Cycling is the best way to explore Yangshuo's countryside. Rent e-bikes (50¥/day) and explore back roads. Follow rice paddies and karst peaks. Return before dark.

culturalTips.title: Yangshuo Escape
culturalTips.content: The Li River cruise to Yangshuo is magical but crowded. Consider staying overnight in Yangshuo and exploring by bike. The Western Street comes alive at night with hundreds of food stalls. Travelers from around the world gather here.

culturalTips.title: Li River History
culturalTips.content: The Li River has been a source of artistic inspiration for over 1000 years. Many famous Chinese paintings and poems depict its karst scenery. The river was once a major transportation route connecting Guangxi with the outside world.

culturalTips.title: Zhuang Culture
culturalTips.content: The Zhuang people are China's largest minority ethnic group. Their traditional Brocade weaving and folk songs (Ge Xiao) are famous throughout China. The Zhuang New Year (Spring Festival) is celebrated with unique customs including water splashing.

culturalTips.title: Yao People
culturalTips.content: The Yao people are known for their Red Detachment of Women costume and traditional Panwang Festival. Women traditionally wear their hair long - some Yao women never cut their hair and wrap it in elaborate coils. Longji villages are authentic Yao communities.

culturalTips.title: Karst Landscape Formation
culturalTips.content: Guilin's karst landscape formed over millions of years as rainwater dissolved limestone bedrock. The same geological process created the caves, underground rivers, and natural bridges. Reed Flute Cave stalactites are estimated to be 450,000 years old.

culturalTips.title: City Name Origin
culturalTips.content: Guilin means 'Forest of Osmanthus' in Chinese. The city is famous for its osmanthus trees which bloom in autumn, filling the air with sweet fragrance. The osmanthus flower is also used in local cuisine - cakes, tea, and wine.

culturalTips.title: Traditional Cormorant Fishing
culturalTips.content: The ancient technique of fishing with trained cormorants is still practiced on the Li River. Fishermen use bamboo rafts and allow cormorants to dive for fish. A ring around the bird's neck prevents them from swallowing large fish.

culturalTips.title: Terrace Farming
culturalTips.content: The Longji Rice Terraces were carved over 600 years by hand, following the contour lines of the mountains. Local farmers still plant rice using traditional methods. Water flows down from the mountains, creating a natural irrigation system.

culturalTips.title: Liu Sanjie Legend
culturalTips.content: Liu Sanjie (Third Sister Liu) is a legendary Zhuang folk singer whose songs have been passed down for centuries. The famous 'Impression Liu Sanjie' outdoor show by Zhang Yimou is set on the Li River where the legend takes place.

culturalTips.title: Guilin Cuisine
culturalTips.content: Guilin cuisine is known for its use of chili peppers, sour flavors, and fresh ingredients. Signature dishes include Guilin rice noodles, beer fish, and various pork preparations. The city is also famous for osmanthus-flavored sweets.

culturalTips.title: Moon Hill Mystery
culturalTips.content: Moon Hill is a natural arch formed by karst erosion, creating a perfect moon shape when viewed from certain angles. The arch is 410m above ground level. Local legend says it was formed by the goddess Chang'e visiting the area.

culturalTips.title: Bamboo Raft History
culturalTips.content: Bamboo rafts have been used on the Li River for over 1000 years as the primary transportation method. Today, they serve mainly tourism but the design remains traditional. A typical raft uses 5-7 bamboo poles lashed together.

culturalTips.title: Cave Systems
culturalTips.content: Guilin has one of the largest concentrations of karst caves in China, with over 1000 explored. Reed Flute Cave (Ludi Yan) is the most famous, with stalactites illuminated in rainbow colors. Other notable caves include Silver Cave and Gongzhong Cave.

culturalTips.title: Best Photography Spots
culturalTips.content: For classic Guilin shots: Elephant Trunk Hill at sunrise, Longji terraces at golden hour, Yulong River bamboo rafts in morning mist. The 20 RMB note features Elephant Trunk Hill and the Five-Hundred-Bhon Temple.

culturalTips.title: Guilin Tea Culture
culturalTips.content: Lipu tea and Gulong tea are famous local varieties. Tea houses are popular gathering spots. The traditional tea ceremony involves multiple infusions, each with different flavor notes. Tea is often served with osmanthus cakes.

culturalTips.title: Guilin Tea Ceremony
culturalTips.content: Traditional Guangxi tea ceremony involves 'Gongfu Cha' with multiple infusions. Tea houses near Elephant Hill serve osmanthus tea alongside the view. The tea culture here combines Zhuang and Han traditions.

culturalTips.title: Zhuang Water Splashing Festival
culturalTips.content: The Zhuang New Year includes '泼水节' (Water Splashing Festival) where locals splash each other and visitors with water for blessing. Spring is the main season. Joining is acceptable - expect to get wet!

culturalTips.title: Bamboo Raft Photography Tips
culturalTips.content: For the best bamboo raft photos, go early morning when mist rises. The Yulong River bamboo rafts are more scenic than Li River. Wear a hat and sunscreen - there's no shade. Cameras should be protected from splashes.

culturalTips.title: Best Seasons for Rice Terraces
culturalTips.content: Longji terraces look different each season: water-filled in spring (April-May), green in summer, golden in autumn (September-October), snowy in winter. Spring is most dramatic but wet. Autumn is most popular.

culturalTips.title: Yangshuo English Level
culturalTips.content: Yangshuo has the highest concentration of English speakers outside major cities in China. Many restaurant menus have English. Market vendors use calculators for prices. Still, learning '你好' (hello) and '多少钱' (how much) appreciated.

culturalTips.title: Bamboo Shoot Season
culturalTips.content: Fresh bamboo shoots (竹笋) are a spring delicacy in Guilin. They're harvested from March to May and appear in many local dishes. Try '酸笋' (sour bamboo shoots) - an acquired taste. They're also sold dried year-round.

culturalTips.title: Li River Cruise Tips
culturalTips.content: The classic 4-hour cruise from Zhujiang to Yangshuo passes iconic scenery. Book upper deck for best views. Lunch included on board. Evening cruises available for sunset. Bring layers - river can be cold.

culturalTips.title: Zhuang Language and Customs
culturalTips.content: The Zhuang language is tonal and different from Mandarin. Their folk songs 'Ge Xiao' are UNESCO intangible heritage. When visiting Zhuang homes, remove shoes at door. Tipping head chef shows respect. 'Gai' means 'chicken' in Zhuang dialect.

culturalTips.title: Karst Cave Safety
culturalTips.content: Guilin's caves are cool year-round (15-18C). Wear layers and comfortable shoes. Paths can be slippery. Watch your head on low stalactites. The 'Lightning Tower' in Silver Cave is 26m high. Photography restricted in some areas.

culturalTips.title: Osmanthus Blossom Festival
culturalTips.content: Guilin's osmanthus trees bloom in late September to early October, filling the city with sweet fragrance. The Osmanthus Festival includes cultural performances, tea ceremonies, and osmanthus-themed foods. Book accommodation early for this period.

culturalTips.title: Yangshuo Nightlife Scene
culturalTips.content: Yangshuo has a surprisingly vibrant nightlife for its size. West Street has live music bars, rooftop pubs, and foreign-owned cafes. '印象刘三姐' (Impression Liu Sanjie) show starts at 20:00. Karaoke and bar hopping popular among locals.

culturalTips.title: Fishing Village Life
culturalTips.content: Life in Yangshuo's fishing villages along the river remains traditional. Cormorant fishing, bamboo rafts, and net mending are daily activities. Some families offer homestays - a chance to experience authentic river life. Early morning is most photogenic.

culturalTips.title: E-Bike Rental in Yangshuo
culturalTips.content: Rent e-bikes at shops along West Street (30-50¥/day). No license needed for under-50cc vehicles. Follow traffic rules - bikes stay right. Helmet recommended but not always available. Return before dark as roads unlit.

culturalTips.title: River Fish Preparation Methods
culturalTips.content: Guilin's river fish (鲤鱼, 草鱼) prepared various ways: '啤酒鱼' (beer fish - braised with tomato and beer), '漓江蒸鱼' (steamed), '炭烤鱼' (grilled). Ask for '少辣' (less spicy) if needed. Freshness guaranteed in riverside restaurants.

emergencyContacts.name: 澳大利亚驻广州总领事馆
emergencyContacts.notes: Nearest consular services - some countries may not have representation in Guilin

emergencyContacts.name: 桂林旅游警察
emergencyContacts.notes: English speaking tourist police in scenic areas

emergencyContacts.name: 漓江搜救中心
emergencyContacts.notes: For water emergencies and river rescue

emergencyContacts.name: 澳大利亚驻广州总领事馆
加拿大驻广州总领事馆

quickFacts.visa: Most nationalities require visa. 144h transit visa available at airport.
quickFacts.water: Use bottled water - local water may upset stomach
